name: Harvest Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and credit products with local branches in Minnesota.
description_long:
Harvest Bank is a community-focused FDIC-insured financial institution operating in Minnesota with branch locations in Kimball, St. Augusta, Kandiyohi, and Atwater. The bank emphasizes local banking principles, stating on their website that they "support local businesses, farms, and individuals to grow and thrive." Founded to serve their communities, Harvest Bank positions itself as an alternative to larger national banks by providing personalized service and deep community involvement.

Harvest Bank offers a comprehensive range of consumer and business banking products. On the personal side, they provide multiple checking account options (Free Kasasa Cash Checking, Free Kasasa Cash Back Checking, Free Checking, and Investors Checking), savings accounts (Kasasa Saver, Investors Savings Account, Money Market Account), Certificates of Deposit, Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs). They issue MasterCard debit cards, Visa prepaid and gift cards, and Visa credit cards. Their lending products include personal loans, auto loans, home mortgage loans, home equity loans, and home equity lines of credit (HELOC). For business customers, they offer business checking accounts, business savings, business money market accounts, business credit cards, business debit cards, business term loans, Small Business Administration (SBA) loans, and agricultural/agri-business loans.

Harvest Bank differentiates itself through specialized agricultural financing programs, including operating loans, lines of credit, equipment financing, and land loans—reflecting the rural and agricultural character of their service areas. They participate in three free ATM networks to provide surcharge-free access. Digital services include online banking, mobile banking with mobile deposit capability, a Brella fraud protection app, eStatements, digital wallet integration, and direct connectivity with Quicken and QuickBooks. They also support community initiatives, as evidenced by their food drive donations mentioned on their homepage.

As a small regional bank with four branch locations, Harvest Bank's reach is geographically limited to central Minnesota. While they offer competitive products comparable to larger banks, customers should verify that local branch access and specific product rates meet their needs. Their emphasis on agriculture and small business lending may mean less specialized expertise in other niches. The bank's strength lies in community relationships and personalized service rather than the product breadth or digital innovation of larger financial institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (Free Kasasa Cash, Free Kasasa Cash Back, Free Checking, Investors Checking)
Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Health Savings Accounts (HSAs) for personal and business
Home mortgage loans, home equity loans, and home equity lines of credit (HELOC)
Personal loans, auto loans, and consumer loans
Business checking accounts and business savings accounts
Agricultural and agri-business loans (operating loans, equipment financing, land loans)
Small Business Administration (SBA) loans and commercial real estate loans
Mobile banking with mobile deposit, online banking, and bill pay
MasterCard debit cards and Visa credit cards (personal and business)
Merchant card services, ACH processing, and remote deposit capture for business
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Multiple free checking options including Kasasa Cash Checking with rewards features
Membership in three free ATM networks for surcharge-free cash access
Specialized agricultural financing programs including operating loans, equipment financing, and land loans
Mobile banking with mobile deposit capture capability for remote check deposits
Diverse lending products including home loans, auto loans, personal loans, and SBA loans
Community-focused approach with local branch presence in four Minnesota locations
cons: Geographic service area limited to central Minnesota (Kimball, St. Augusta, Kandiyohi, Atwater only)
No indication of digital-only account opening or fully remote banking capabilities mentioned on website
Limited product information on specific APRs, fees, and rate comparisons available on public website
Small regional bank may have less extensive online resources and technology features compared to national banks
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or insurance products beyond basic banking
best_for: Small business owners and farmers seeking specialized agricultural lending and community banking relationships
Central Minnesota residents who value local branch access and personalized service over digital-first banking
Individuals seeking multiple free checking account options with rewards (Kasasa) features
Customers interested in comprehensive lending products (home, auto, personal) from a single institution
diagnosis: Harvest Bank is best for small business owners, farmers, and central Minnesota residents who prioritize local relationships and community banking over digital convenience. The main caveat is geographic limitation—their four-branch footprint means customers must be in their Minnesota service areas to fully utilize their in-person services and community focus.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
